hero.subtitle: Native performance, minimal latency and immaculate DX. Built in Go for devs who ship the whole stack — from the database to the pixel.
hero.platforms: macOS · Linux · Windows · ARM and x86_64

0.title: Cold start in 142ms
0.text: Core written in Go. Native rendering, incremental indexing and zero Electron overhead — opens huge projects without choking.

1.title: Shortcuts forged for flow
1.text: Command Center, smart multi-cursor and cross-stack Go-to-Definition — TypeScript, Go, Rust or SQL in a single session.

2.title: Frontend and backend, side by side
2.text: Bundled LSPs, integrated terminal, git/GitHub and file watcher — your entire stack without installing twelve extensions in week one.

features.subtitle: LSP, terminal, git and search are tuned out of the box. Open the project and start — no plugin hunting, no configuring three different paths for the same formatter.

row1.text: LSPs managed by Adila, with hot-reload on schema changes. TypeScript, Go, Rust, Python and SQL talk to each other — Go-to-Definition crosses the stack.

row2.title2: git that stays out of the way.
row2.text: Multiple shell sessions with prompt integration, inline git status and signed commits without leaving the editor. Push, PR and review in three keystrokes.

powerFeatures.subtitle: Every Adila feature solves a concrete pain for engineers maintaining an entire stack in production.

0.title: Command Center
0.text: A single shortcut for files, symbols, commands and git history. Fuzzy ranking trained on how you actually use it.

1.title: Managed LSP
1.text: Servers installed on demand. Background updates — no terminal required.

2.title: Git and GitHub
2.text: Inline diff, blame, integrated PRs and reviews. Guided stash and cherry-pick with a history you can actually read.

3.title: Multi-shell terminal
3.text: bash, zsh, fish or pwsh with prompt integration and native split panes. Sub-8ms key latency.

performance.title: Speed isn't a detail.
performance.subtitle: Adila is measured and re-measured on every release. These are real numbers — not marketing estimates.

releases.title: Release notes.
releases.subtitle: Everything that landed in each Adila release — straight from the RELEASES.md in the repo.

cta.subtitle: Public beta. Free while it lasts. Your configs travel with your account.
cta.heading: Download Adila
cta.text: A single binary. We auto-detect your platform on the next screen.

roadmap.title: What's coming.
roadmap.subtitle: Adila's public planning — refreshed on every release.

items.1: Refactor: symbolic cross-file rename via LSP
items.2: GitHub Actions log streaming in the sidebar

items.1: Notebook mode for Markdown + executable code
items.2: Test panels with agnostic runner (vitest, go test, cargo test)

items.1: Inline AI, opt-in, with a configurable model
items.2: WebAssembly extensions in a safe sandbox

docs.title: Learn Adila in an afternoon.
docs.subtitle: The official guide: install, configuration, shortcuts and everything you need to ship.

start.title: Getting started
start.text: Install, first project and a tour of the UI. Five minutes from zero to first commit.

config.title: Configuration
config.text: JSON settings, per-workspace profiles and how to sync between machines.

lsp.title: LSPs
lsp.text: How to enable Go, Rust, TypeScript, Python and SQL — including custom servers.

keys.title: Shortcuts
keys.text: Full keybindings reference, with presets for vim, emacs and VS Code.

themes.title: Themes
themes.text: Dark, light and high-contrast. How to build and share your own.

git.title: Git and GitHub
git.text: Diff, blame, branch flows, signed PRs and what's integrated out of the box.

cli.title: adila, the command.
cli.subtitle: Use Adila from the terminal: open projects, run diff and manage the install.

commands.open: Opens the current folder in a new Adila window.
commands.diff: Compares two files side by side inside Adila.
commands.install: Adds the `adila` binary to your PATH.
commands.uninstall: Removes the `adila` binary from your PATH.
commands.version: Shows the installed version and build hash.

about.title: Built by people who use it.
about.subtitle: Adila was born from a simple frustration: editors that don't respect the speed of a fullstack dev.

mission.title: Mission
mission.text: Build the fastest editor for engineers who maintain frontend, backend and infrastructure in the same day.

body.0: Adila is an independent project — no venture capital, no hidden agenda. The core is open-source on GitHub and the roadmap is public.
body.1: We believe developer tools should be predictable: keybindings that don't shift release after release, low latency on any machine, and config you can read.
body.2: Everything on this landing is in public beta — feedback and issues are especially welcome at this stage.

privacy.title: What we collect. And what we don't.
privacy.subtitle: Short, plain-language policy. If anything here changes, we update the date and tell you.

collect.title: What we collect
collect.text: Optional crash and performance telemetry, off by default. When enabled, we send only Adila version, OS and an anonymized stack trace. No file content is ever collected.

store.title: Where we store it
store.text: Settings live on disk in your system data directory. Nothing leaves your machine without explicit action, like the optional config sync tied to your account.

share.title: Sharing
share.text: We do not sell, rent or trade data with third parties. Infrastructure providers process data strictly to deliver the service (sync, updates, telemetry).

rights.title: Your rights
rights.text: You can export, correct or delete your data anytime via Settings or by reaching out. Applicable law: LGPD (Brazil) and GDPR (EU) where it applies.

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: Questions, deletion or audit requests: privacy@adila.dev. We answer within 7 days.

license.title: Open-source, MIT.

footer.tagline: Adila is a native code editor built in Go. Performance, DX and fullstack — no compromises.
